slides.title: Validation vs Sanitization
slides.content:
# Two Steps

*   **Validation**: Check if the data meets certain criteria (e.g., email format, required fields).
*   **Sanitization**: Clean the data to make it safe (e.g., trim spaces, escape HTML).

Always validate and sanitize before using data.

slides.title: Common Validation Functions
Validation Example
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$errors = [];
if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] === 'POST') {
    $name = trim($_POST['name'] ?? '');
    $email = trim($_POST['email'] ?? '');
    if (empty($name)) {
        $errors[] = "Name is required.";
    }
    if (!filter_var($email, FILTER_VALIDATE_EMAIL)) {
        $errors[] = "Invalid email format.";
    }
    if (empty($errors)) {
        // process
    }
}
?>

slides.title: Sanitization Functions
slides.content:
# Cleaning Input

*   `trim()` – remove whitespace from ends
*   `strip_tags()` – remove HTML and PHP tags
*   `htmlspecialchars()` – convert special characters to HTML entities (prevents XSS)
*   `filter_var()` with sanitization filters: `FILTER_SANITIZE_EMAIL`, `FILTER_SANITIZE_URL`, `FILTER_SANITIZE_STRING` (deprecated)

When outputting user data to HTML, always use `htmlspecialchars()`:
```php
echo htmlspecialchars($user_input, ENT_QUOTES, 'UTF-8');
```

quizData.question: Which function should you use to prevent XSS when outputting user data?
quizData.explanation: htmlspecialchars converts special characters to HTML entities, preventing XSS. (FILTER_SANITIZE_STRING is deprecated.)
